4	import dataclasses
5	from collections import OrderedDict, defaultdict
6	from dataclasses import dataclass
7	from enum import Enum, auto
8	from types import MappingProxyType
9	from typing import Tuple, Dict, Set, Optional
10	
11	from cuda.tile._ir.type import PartitionViewTy, TokenTy, ArrayValue
12	from cuda.tile._memory_model import MemoryOrder
13	from cuda.tile._exception import Loc, TileInternalError
14	from cuda.tile._ir.ir import Block, IRContext, Var, Operation, MemoryEffect
15	from cuda.tile._ir.control_flow_ops import Break, Continue, EndBranch, IfElse, Loop
16	from cuda.tile._ir.ops import (
17	    JoinTokens, MakeToken,
18	    TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, LoadPointer, TileAtomicRedView,
19	    TileLoad, StorePointer,
20	    TileStore, TileAssert, TilePrintf,
21	)
22	from cuda.tile._ir.ops_utils import memory_order_has_acquire, memory_order_has_release
23	from cuda.tile._passes.dataflow_analysis import ALIAS_UNIVERSE, DataflowResult, AliasSet
24	
25	
26	class MemoryEffects:
27	    def __init__(self,
28	                 array_mem_effects: OrderedDict[AliasSet, MemoryEffect],
29	                 has_acquire_order: bool):
30	        self._array_mem_effects = MappingProxyType(array_mem_effects)
31	        self._has_acquire_order = has_acquire_order
32	
33	    @property
34	    def has_acquire_order(self) -> bool:
35	        return self._has_acquire_order
36	
37	    def items(self):
38	        return self._array_mem_effects.items()
39	
40	    def __getitem__(self, alias_name: AliasSet):
41	        return self._array_mem_effects.get(alias_name, MemoryEffect.NONE)
42	
43	    def __or__(self, other) -> "MemoryEffects":
44	        if not isinstance(other, MemoryEffects):
45	            return NotImplemented
46	
47	        union_effects = OrderedDict(self._array_mem_effects)
48	        for a in other._array_mem_effects.keys():
49	            union_effects[a] = max(self[a], other[a])
50	        return MemoryEffects(union_effects, self.has_acquire_order | other.has_acquire_order)
51	
52	
53	EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS = MemoryEffects(OrderedDict(), False)
54	
55	
56	class AcquireTokenKeyClass:
57	    def __repr__(self):
58	        return "ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY"
59	
60	
61	ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY = AcquireTokenKeyClass()
62	
63	
64	class TokenRole(Enum):
65	    LAST_OP = auto()
66	    LAST_STORE = auto()
67	
68	
69	@dataclass(frozen=True)
70	class AliasTokenKey:
71	    alias_set: AliasSet
72	    role: TokenRole
73	
74	
75	TokenKey = AliasTokenKey | AcquireTokenKeyClass
76	
77	
78	@dataclass(frozen=True)
79	class IfElseInfo:
80	    ifelse_mem_effects: MemoryEffects
81	
82	
83	@dataclass(frozen=True)
84	class InnermostLoopInfo:
85	    loop_mem_effects: MemoryEffects
86	    # TODO: remove once parallel loop store no longer depends on these two
87	    loop_parallel_stores: Set[Operation]
88	    parent_token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var]
89	
90	
91	@dataclass(frozen=True)
92	class VarInfo:
93	    root_var: Dict[str, str]
94	
95	
96	@dataclass(frozen=True)
97	class TokenOrderContext:
98	    dataflow_result: DataflowResult
99	    block_memory_effects: Dict[Block, MemoryEffects]
100	
101	
102	def token_order_pass(root_block: Block, dataflow_result: DataflowResult):
103	    block_memory_effects = {}
104	    _get_block_memory_effects(root_block, dataflow_result, block_memory_effects)
105	    context = TokenOrderContext(dataflow_result, block_memory_effects)
106	
107	    root_tok = _make_token_var(root_block.ctx, root_block.loc)
108	    token_map = defaultdict(lambda: root_tok)
109	    _to_token_order_in_block(root_block, context, token_map)
110	    root_block[:0] = (MakeToken(result_vars=(root_tok,), loc=root_block.loc),)
111	
112	
113	def _get_input_var(op: Operation):
114	    if "view" in op.operands:
115	        array_value = op.view.get_aggregate()
116	        assert isinstance(array_value, ArrayValue)
117	        return array_value.base_ptr
118	    elif "pointer" in op.operands:
119	        return op.pointer
120	    else:
121	        raise TileInternalError(f"Cannot determine input var for op {op}: "
122	                                f"expected 'view' or 'pointer' operand")
123	
124	
125	def _get_block_memory_effects(block: Block,
126	                              dataflow_result: DataflowResult,
127	                              block_memory_effects: Dict[Block, MemoryEffects]):
128	
129	    def get_memory_effects(cur_op):
130	        effect = cur_op.memory_effect
131	        if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE or isinstance(cur_op, TileAssert):
132	            return EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS
133	
134	        if isinstance(cur_op, TilePrintf):
135	
136	            return MemoryEffects(OrderedDict([(ALIAS_UNIVERSE, effect)]), False)
137	
138	        has_acquire_order = False
139	        if isinstance(cur_op, (TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, TileAtomicRedView,
140	                               TileLoad, TileStore)):
141	            has_acquire_order = memory_order_has_acquire(cur_op.memory_order)
142	
143	        return MemoryEffects({dataflow_result[_get_input_var(cur_op).name].alias_set: effect},
144	                             has_acquire_order)
145	
146	    blk_mem_effects = EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS
147	    for op in block.operations:
148	        blk_mem_effects = blk_mem_effects | get_memory_effects(op)
149	        # Include nested blocks' memory effects in parent block
150	        for nested_block in op.nested_blocks:
151	            _get_block_memory_effects(nested_block, dataflow_result, block_memory_effects)
152	            blk_mem_effects = blk_mem_effects | block_memory_effects[nested_block]
153	
154	    block_memory_effects[block] = blk_mem_effects
155	
156	
157	def _to_token_order_in_block(block: Block,
158	                             context: TokenOrderContext,
159	                             token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
160	                             *,
161	                             innermost_loop_info: Optional[InnermostLoopInfo] = None,
162	                             ifelse_info: Optional[IfElseInfo] = None,):
163	    operations = []
164	
165	    # Convert the old ops to token ordered ops,
166	    # including control flow ops containing token ordered ops
167	    for op in block.operations:
168	        if isinstance(op, (TileLoad, LoadPointer)):
169	            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
170	            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
171	            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
172	
173	            mem_order = op.memory_order if isinstance(op, TileLoad) else None
174	            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_store_key, op,
175	                                                                  token_map, mem_order,
176	                                                                  block.ctx)
177	            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
178	                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)
179	
180	            # Convert
181	            _, result_tok = op.result_vars
182	            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))
183	
184	            # Eagerly join with last_op_token
185	            new_last_op_tok = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
186	            join_op = JoinTokens(tokens=(token_map[last_op_key], result_tok),
187	                                 result_vars=(new_last_op_tok,), loc=op.loc)
188	            operations.append(join_op)
189	
190	            token_map[last_op_key] = new_last_op_tok
191	
192	            if isinstance(op, TileLoad) and memory_order_has_acquire(op.memory_order):
193	                token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = result_tok
194	
195	        elif isinstance(op, (TileStore, StorePointer)):
196	            # Try to parallelize the store in the innermost loop if possible
197	            if (
198	                isinstance(op, TileStore)
199	                and (parallel_store_ops := _try_loop_parallel_store(op, context.dataflow_result,
200	                                                                    token_map, innermost_loop_info,
201	                                                                    block.ctx))
202	            ):
203	                operations.extend(parallel_store_ops)
204	                continue
205	
206	            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
207	            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
208	            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
209	
210	            mem_order = op.memory_order if isinstance(op, TileStore) else None
211	            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_op_key, op, token_map,
212	                                                                  mem_order, block.ctx)
213	            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
214	                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)
215	
216	            [result_tok] = op.result_vars
217	            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))
218	
219	            token_map[last_op_key] = result_tok
220	            token_map[last_store_key] = result_tok
221	
222	        elif isinstance(op, (TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, TileAtomicRedView)):
223	            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
224	            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
225	            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
226	
227	            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_op_key, op, token_map,
228	                                                                  op.memory_order, block.ctx)
229	            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
230	                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)
231	
232	            if isinstance(op, TileAtomicRedView):
233	                [result_tok] = op.result_vars
234	            else:
235	                _, result_tok = op.result_vars
236	
237	            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))
238	
239	            token_map[last_op_key] = result_tok
240	            token_map[last_store_key] = result_tok
241	
242	            if memory_order_has_acquire(op.memory_order):
243	                token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = result_tok
244	
245	        elif isinstance(op, Loop):
246	            body_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[op.body]
247	
248	            new_initial_values = list(op.initial_values)
249	            new_body_params = list(op.body.params)
250	            new_result_vars = list(op.result_vars)
251	
252	            def append_new_carried_var(init_var: Var):
253	                new_initial_values.append(init_var)
254	                body_var = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
255	                new_body_params.append(body_var)
256	                res_var = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
257	                new_result_vars.append(res_var)
258	                return body_var, res_var
259	
260	            result_token_map = token_map.copy()
261	            body_token_map = token_map.copy()
262	            for alias_set, effect in body_mem_effects.items():
263	                last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
264	                last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
265	
266	                if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE:
267	                    continue
268	                elif effect == MemoryEffect.LOAD:
269	                    body_token_map[last_op_key], result_token_map[last_op_key] = \
270	                        append_new_carried_var(token_map[last_op_key])
271	                elif effect == MemoryEffect.STORE:
272	                    body_token_map[last_op_key], result_token_map[last_op_key] = \
273	                        append_new_carried_var(token_map[last_op_key])
274	                    body_token_map[last_store_key], result_token_map[last_store_key] = \
275	                        append_new_carried_var(token_map[last_store_key])
276	                else:
277	                    raise TileInternalError(f"Unexpected memory effect: {effect}")
278	
279	            if body_mem_effects.has_acquire_order:
280	                body_token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY], result_token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = \
281	                    append_new_carried_var(token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY])
282	
283	            # Get parallel stores in the body block
284	            parallel_stores = _get_parallel_stores(op, context)
285	
286	            _to_token_order_in_block(op.body, context, body_token_map,
287	                                     ifelse_info=None,
288	                                     innermost_loop_info=InnermostLoopInfo(body_mem_effects,
289	                                                                           parallel_stores,
290	                                                                           token_map))
291	
292	            op.body.params = tuple(new_body_params)
293	            new_loop_op = dataclasses.replace(op, initial_values=tuple(new_initial_values),
294	                                              result_vars=tuple(new_result_vars))
295	            operations.append(new_loop_op)
296	
297	            token_map = result_token_map
298	
299	        elif isinstance(op, Continue):
300	            tokens = _get_cf_exit_tokens(innermost_loop_info.loop_mem_effects, token_map)
301	
302	            new_continue_op = dataclasses.replace(op, values=tuple(op.values) + tokens)
303	            operations.append(new_continue_op)
304	
305	        elif isinstance(op, Break):
306	            tokens = _get_cf_exit_tokens(innermost_loop_info.loop_mem_effects, token_map)
307	
308	            new_break_op = dataclasses.replace(op, values=tuple(op.values) + tokens)
309	            operations.append(new_break_op)
310	
311	        elif isinstance(op, IfElse):
312	            # Merge memory effects from both branches
313	            then_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[op.then_block]
314	            else_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[op.else_block]
315	            merged_mem_effects = then_mem_effects | else_mem_effects
316	
317	            result_token_map = token_map.copy()
318	            new_result_vars = list(op.result_vars)
319	
320	            def add_new_ifelse_result():
321	                x = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
322	                new_result_vars.append(x)
323	                return x
324	
325	            for alias_set, effect in merged_mem_effects.items():
326	                last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
327	                last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
328	
329	                if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE:
330	                    continue
331	                elif effect == MemoryEffect.LOAD:
332	                    result_token_map[last_op_key] = add_new_ifelse_result()
333	                elif effect == MemoryEffect.STORE:
334	                    result_token_map[last_op_key] = add_new_ifelse_result()
335	                    result_token_map[last_store_key] = add_new_ifelse_result()
336	                else:
337	                    raise TileInternalError(f"Unexpected memory effect: {effect}")
338	
339	            if merged_mem_effects.has_acquire_order:
340	                result_token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = add_new_ifelse_result()
341	
342	            # Branch to then and else blocks
343	            for nested_block in op.nested_blocks:
344	                _to_token_order_in_block(nested_block, context, token_map.copy(),
345	                                         innermost_loop_info=innermost_loop_info,
346	                                         ifelse_info=IfElseInfo(merged_mem_effects))
347	
348	            new_ifelse_op = dataclasses.replace(op, result_vars=tuple(new_result_vars))
349	            operations.append(new_ifelse_op)
350	
351	            token_map = result_token_map
352	
353	        elif isinstance(op, EndBranch):
354	            tokens = _get_cf_exit_tokens(ifelse_info.ifelse_mem_effects, token_map)
355	
356	            new_end_branch_op = dataclasses.replace(op, outputs=tuple(op.outputs) + tokens)
357	            operations.append(new_end_branch_op)
358	
359	        elif isinstance(op, TilePrintf):
360	            # Use ALIAS_UNIVERSE here to order print. This is a conservative solution that will use
361	            # the existing boop/branch token plumbing to preserve token order. Doing so could order
362	            # unrelated memory chains, which is acceptable for now.
363	            alias_set = ALIAS_UNIVERSE
364	            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
365	            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
366	
367	            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_op_key, op, token_map,
368	                                                                  None, block.ctx)
369	            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
370	                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)
371	
372	            [result_tok] = op.result_vars
373	            token_map[last_op_key] = result_tok
374	            token_map[last_store_key] = result_tok
375	            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))
376	        else:
377	            operations.append(op)
378	
379	    block.operations = operations
380	
381	
382	def _last_op_key(alias_set: AliasSet):
383	    return AliasTokenKey(alias_set, TokenRole.LAST_OP)
384	
385	
386	def _last_store_key(alias_set: AliasSet):
387	    return AliasTokenKey(alias_set, TokenRole.LAST_STORE)
388	
389	
390	def _make_token_var(ir_ctx: IRContext, loc: Loc) -> Var:
391	    var = ir_ctx.make_var("$token", loc)
392	    var.set_type(TokenTy())
393	    return var
394	
395	
396	def _collect_join_tokens(token_key: TokenKey,
397	                         token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
398	                         memory_order: MemoryOrder | None):
399	
400	    def should_join(other_key):
401	        if other_key == ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY:
402	            return True
403	
404	        assert isinstance(other_key, AliasTokenKey)
405	
406	        mem_order_release_join = (memory_order is not None and
407	                                  memory_order_has_release(memory_order) and
408	                                  other_key.role == TokenRole.LAST_OP)
409	        alias_set_overlap_join = (other_key.role == token_key.role and
410	                                  (other_key.alias_set & token_key.alias_set))
411	        return mem_order_release_join or alias_set_overlap_join
412	
413	    # Preserve the order of the tokens to join
414	    tokens_to_join = [token_map[token_key]]
415	    for other_key, other_tok in token_map.items():
416	        if not should_join(other_key):
417	            continue
418	        if other_tok not in tokens_to_join:
419	            tokens_to_join.append(other_tok)
420	
421	    return tokens_to_join
422	
423	
424	def _get_input_token(token_key: TokenKey,
425	                     op: Operation,
426	                     token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
427	                     memory_order: MemoryOrder | None,
428	                     ctx: IRContext) -> Tuple[Var, Operation | None]:
429	    tokens_to_join = _collect_join_tokens(token_key, token_map, memory_order)
430	
431	    if len(tokens_to_join) == 1:
432	        return tokens_to_join[0], None
433	
434	    ret_tok = _make_token_var(ctx, op.loc)
435	    ret_op = JoinTokens(tokens=tuple(tokens_to_join), result_vars=(ret_tok,), loc=op.loc)
436	    return ret_tok, ret_op
437	
438	
439	def _get_cf_exit_tokens(cf_mem_effects: MemoryEffects,
440	                        token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var]) -> Tuple[Var, ...]:
441	    tokens = []
442	    for alias_set, effect in cf_mem_effects.items():
443	        last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
444	        last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
445	
446	        if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE:
447	            continue
448	        if effect == MemoryEffect.LOAD:
449	            tokens.append(token_map[last_op_key])
450	        elif effect == MemoryEffect.STORE:
451	            tokens.extend((token_map[last_op_key], token_map[last_store_key]))
452	
453	    if cf_mem_effects.has_acquire_order:
454	        tokens.append(token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY])
455	
456	    return tuple(tokens)
457	
458	
459	# === LOOP PARALLEL STORE OPTIMIZATION ===
460	
461	
462	def _get_parallel_stores(
463	    loop_op: Loop,
464	    context: TokenOrderContext
465	) -> Set[Operation]:
466	    """
467	    A specific optimization for when there's only one TileStore
468	    on a given array in the for-loop body (including nested blocks),
469	    and the index doesn't overlap across iterations.
470	    We can parallelize the TileStore.
471	
472	    Common in LayerNorm and RMSNorm patterns.
473	    """
474	
475	    if not loop_op.is_for_loop:
476	        return set()
477	
478	    # Skips this optimization if alias_set size > 1 is present in the loop body
479	    body_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[loop_op.body]
480	    if any(alias_set == ALIAS_UNIVERSE or alias_set.bit_count() > 1
481	           for alias_set, _ in body_mem_effects.items()):
482	        return set()
483	
484	    nested_mem_effects = _get_nested_mem_effects(loop_op, context.block_memory_effects)
485	
486	    alias_set_to_mem_ops = defaultdict(list)
487	    for op in loop_op.body.operations:
488	        if isinstance(op, (TileLoad, StorePointer, LoadPointer, TileStore,
489	                           TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, TileAtomicRedView)):
490	            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
491	            alias_set_to_mem_ops[alias_set].append(op)
492	
493	    tile_store_candidates = set()
494	    for alias_set, mem_ops in alias_set_to_mem_ops.items():
495	        if len(mem_ops) != 1:
496	            # More than 1 memory ops on the same array in loop
497	            continue
498	        elif not isinstance(mem_ops[0], TileStore):
499	            # The memory op on array is not TileStore
500	            continue
501	        elif nested_mem_effects[alias_set] != MemoryEffect.NONE:
502	            # The nested blocks / func calls have memory effects on array
503	            continue
504	        tile_store_candidates.add(mem_ops[0])
505	
506	    # Filter in stores that have non-overlapping indices
507	    res = _filter_by_store_index(loop_op, tile_store_candidates, context.dataflow_result)
508	    return res
509	
510	
511	def _get_nested_mem_effects(
512	    loop_op: Loop,
513	    block_memory_effects: Dict[Block, MemoryEffects]
514	) -> MemoryEffects:
515	    nested_blocks = [b for op in loop_op.body.operations for b in op.nested_blocks]
516	    nested_mem_effects = EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS
517	    for b in nested_blocks:
518	        nested_mem_effects = nested_mem_effects | block_memory_effects[b]
519	    return nested_mem_effects
520	
521	
522	def _filter_by_store_index(loop_op: Loop,
523	                           tile_store_candidates: Set[Operation],
524	                           dataflow_result: DataflowResult) -> Set[Operation]:
525	
526	    def is_idx_injective(idx_var: Var) -> bool:
527	        # TODO: allow more complex injective check: j = i * 2 + 3
528	        return loop_op.is_for_loop and idx_var.name == loop_op.induction_var.name
529	
530	    ret = set()
531	    for store_op in tile_store_candidates:
532	        ptr = _get_input_var(store_op)
533	        view_ty = store_op.view.get_type()
534	        if isinstance(view_ty, PartitionViewTy) \
535	                and not dataflow_result[ptr.name].may_alias_internally \
536	                and any(is_idx_injective(idx_var) for idx_var in store_op.index):
537	            ret.add(store_op)
538	    return ret
539	
540	
541	def _try_loop_parallel_store(
542	    store_op: TileStore,
543	    dataflow_result: DataflowResult,
544	    token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
545	    innermost_loop_info: Optional[InnermostLoopInfo],
546	    ctx: IRContext,
547	) -> Optional[Tuple[Operation, ...] | Operation]:
548	
549	    if (not innermost_loop_info or
550	            store_op not in innermost_loop_info.loop_parallel_stores):
551	        return None
552	
553	    alias_set = dataflow_result[_get_input_var(store_op).name].alias_set
554	    last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
555	    last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)
556	
557	    # Convert to parallellized store
558	    parent_token_map = innermost_loop_info.parent_token_map
559	    before_loop_last_op_tok = parent_token_map[last_op_key]
560	
561	    if (ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY in token_map and
562	            before_loop_last_op_tok is not token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY]):
563	        input_tok = _make_token_var(ctx, store_op.loc)
564	        maybe_input_tok_join_op = JoinTokens(
565	            tokens=(before_loop_last_op_tok, token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY]),
566	            result_vars=(input_tok,), loc=store_op.loc)
567	    else:
568	        input_tok = before_loop_last_op_tok
569	        maybe_input_tok_join_op = None
570	
571	    [result_tok] = store_op.result_vars
572	    tko_store_op = dataclasses.replace(store_op, token=input_tok)
573	
574	    # Eagerly join with loop_last_op_tok
575	    loop_last_op_tok = token_map[last_op_key]
576	    new_last_op_tok = _make_token_var(ctx, store_op.loc)
577	    join_op = JoinTokens(tokens=(loop_last_op_tok, result_tok),
578	                         result_vars=(new_last_op_tok,), loc=store_op.loc)
579	
580	    token_map[last_op_key] = new_last_op_tok
581	    token_map[last_store_key] = new_last_op_tok
582	
583	    return ((maybe_input_tok_join_op,) if maybe_input_tok_join_op else ()) + (tko_store_op, join_op)
584

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
import dataclasses
from collections import OrderedDict, defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum, auto
from types import MappingProxyType
from typing import Tuple, Dict, Set, Optional

from cuda.tile._ir.type import PartitionViewTy, TokenTy, ArrayValue
from cuda.tile._memory_model import MemoryOrder
from cuda.tile._exception import Loc, TileInternalError
from cuda.tile._ir.ir import Block, IRContext, Var, Operation, MemoryEffect
from cuda.tile._ir.control_flow_ops import Break, Continue, EndBranch, IfElse, Loop
from cuda.tile._ir.ops import (
    JoinTokens, MakeToken,
    TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, LoadPointer, TileAtomicRedView,
    TileLoad, StorePointer,
    TileStore, TileAssert, TilePrintf,
)
from cuda.tile._ir.ops_utils import memory_order_has_acquire, memory_order_has_release
from cuda.tile._passes.dataflow_analysis import ALIAS_UNIVERSE, DataflowResult, AliasSet


class MemoryEffects:
    def __init__(self,
                 array_mem_effects: OrderedDict[AliasSet, MemoryEffect],
                 has_acquire_order: bool):
        self._array_mem_effects = MappingProxyType(array_mem_effects)
        self._has_acquire_order = has_acquire_order

    @property
    def has_acquire_order(self) -> bool:
        return self._has_acquire_order

    def items(self):
        return self._array_mem_effects.items()

    def __getitem__(self, alias_name: AliasSet):
        return self._array_mem_effects.get(alias_name, MemoryEffect.NONE)

    def __or__(self, other) -> "MemoryEffects":
        if not isinstance(other, MemoryEffects):
            return NotImplemented

        union_effects = OrderedDict(self._array_mem_effects)
        for a in other._array_mem_effects.keys():
            union_effects[a] = max(self[a], other[a])
        return MemoryEffects(union_effects, self.has_acquire_order | other.has_acquire_order)


EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS = MemoryEffects(OrderedDict(), False)


class AcquireTokenKeyClass:
    def __repr__(self):
        return "ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY"


ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY = AcquireTokenKeyClass()


class TokenRole(Enum):
    LAST_OP = auto()
    LAST_STORE = auto()


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AliasTokenKey:
    alias_set: AliasSet
    role: TokenRole


TokenKey = AliasTokenKey | AcquireTokenKeyClass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class IfElseInfo:
    ifelse_mem_effects: MemoryEffects


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class InnermostLoopInfo:
    loop_mem_effects: MemoryEffects
    # TODO: remove once parallel loop store no longer depends on these two
    loop_parallel_stores: Set[Operation]
    parent_token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var]


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class VarInfo:
    root_var: Dict[str, str]


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TokenOrderContext:
    dataflow_result: DataflowResult
    block_memory_effects: Dict[Block, MemoryEffects]


def token_order_pass(root_block: Block, dataflow_result: DataflowResult):
    block_memory_effects = {}
    _get_block_memory_effects(root_block, dataflow_result, block_memory_effects)
    context = TokenOrderContext(dataflow_result, block_memory_effects)

    root_tok = _make_token_var(root_block.ctx, root_block.loc)
    token_map = defaultdict(lambda: root_tok)
    _to_token_order_in_block(root_block, context, token_map)
    root_block[:0] = (MakeToken(result_vars=(root_tok,), loc=root_block.loc),)


def _get_input_var(op: Operation):
    if "view" in op.operands:
        array_value = op.view.get_aggregate()
        assert isinstance(array_value, ArrayValue)
        return array_value.base_ptr
    elif "pointer" in op.operands:
        return op.pointer
    else:
        raise TileInternalError(f"Cannot determine input var for op {op}: "
                                f"expected 'view' or 'pointer' operand")


def _get_block_memory_effects(block: Block,
                              dataflow_result: DataflowResult,
                              block_memory_effects: Dict[Block, MemoryEffects]):

    def get_memory_effects(cur_op):
        effect = cur_op.memory_effect
        if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE or isinstance(cur_op, TileAssert):
            return EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS

        if isinstance(cur_op, TilePrintf):

            return MemoryEffects(OrderedDict([(ALIAS_UNIVERSE, effect)]), False)

        has_acquire_order = False
        if isinstance(cur_op, (TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, TileAtomicRedView,
                               TileLoad, TileStore)):
            has_acquire_order = memory_order_has_acquire(cur_op.memory_order)

        return MemoryEffects({dataflow_result[_get_input_var(cur_op).name].alias_set: effect},
                             has_acquire_order)

    blk_mem_effects = EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS
    for op in block.operations:
        blk_mem_effects = blk_mem_effects | get_memory_effects(op)
        # Include nested blocks' memory effects in parent block
        for nested_block in op.nested_blocks:
            _get_block_memory_effects(nested_block, dataflow_result, block_memory_effects)
            blk_mem_effects = blk_mem_effects | block_memory_effects[nested_block]

    block_memory_effects[block] = blk_mem_effects


def _to_token_order_in_block(block: Block,
                             context: TokenOrderContext,
                             token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
                             *,
                             innermost_loop_info: Optional[InnermostLoopInfo] = None,
                             ifelse_info: Optional[IfElseInfo] = None,):
    operations = []

    # Convert the old ops to token ordered ops,
    # including control flow ops containing token ordered ops
    for op in block.operations:
        if isinstance(op, (TileLoad, LoadPointer)):
            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

            mem_order = op.memory_order if isinstance(op, TileLoad) else None
            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_store_key, op,
                                                                  token_map, mem_order,
                                                                  block.ctx)
            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)

            # Convert
            _, result_tok = op.result_vars
            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))

            # Eagerly join with last_op_token
            new_last_op_tok = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
            join_op = JoinTokens(tokens=(token_map[last_op_key], result_tok),
                                 result_vars=(new_last_op_tok,), loc=op.loc)
            operations.append(join_op)

            token_map[last_op_key] = new_last_op_tok

            if isinstance(op, TileLoad) and memory_order_has_acquire(op.memory_order):
                token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = result_tok

        elif isinstance(op, (TileStore, StorePointer)):
            # Try to parallelize the store in the innermost loop if possible
            if (
                isinstance(op, TileStore)
                and (parallel_store_ops := _try_loop_parallel_store(op, context.dataflow_result,
                                                                    token_map, innermost_loop_info,
                                                                    block.ctx))
            ):
                operations.extend(parallel_store_ops)
                continue

            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

            mem_order = op.memory_order if isinstance(op, TileStore) else None
            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_op_key, op, token_map,
                                                                  mem_order, block.ctx)
            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)

            [result_tok] = op.result_vars
            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))

            token_map[last_op_key] = result_tok
            token_map[last_store_key] = result_tok

        elif isinstance(op, (TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, TileAtomicRedView)):
            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_op_key, op, token_map,
                                                                  op.memory_order, block.ctx)
            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)

            if isinstance(op, TileAtomicRedView):
                [result_tok] = op.result_vars
            else:
                _, result_tok = op.result_vars

            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))

            token_map[last_op_key] = result_tok
            token_map[last_store_key] = result_tok

            if memory_order_has_acquire(op.memory_order):
                token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = result_tok

        elif isinstance(op, Loop):
            body_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[op.body]

            new_initial_values = list(op.initial_values)
            new_body_params = list(op.body.params)
            new_result_vars = list(op.result_vars)

            def append_new_carried_var(init_var: Var):
                new_initial_values.append(init_var)
                body_var = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
                new_body_params.append(body_var)
                res_var = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
                new_result_vars.append(res_var)
                return body_var, res_var

            result_token_map = token_map.copy()
            body_token_map = token_map.copy()
            for alias_set, effect in body_mem_effects.items():
                last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
                last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

                if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE:
                    continue
                elif effect == MemoryEffect.LOAD:
                    body_token_map[last_op_key], result_token_map[last_op_key] = \
                        append_new_carried_var(token_map[last_op_key])
                elif effect == MemoryEffect.STORE:
                    body_token_map[last_op_key], result_token_map[last_op_key] = \
                        append_new_carried_var(token_map[last_op_key])
                    body_token_map[last_store_key], result_token_map[last_store_key] = \
                        append_new_carried_var(token_map[last_store_key])
                else:
                    raise TileInternalError(f"Unexpected memory effect: {effect}")

            if body_mem_effects.has_acquire_order:
                body_token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY], result_token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = \
                    append_new_carried_var(token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY])

            # Get parallel stores in the body block
            parallel_stores = _get_parallel_stores(op, context)

            _to_token_order_in_block(op.body, context, body_token_map,
                                     ifelse_info=None,
                                     innermost_loop_info=InnermostLoopInfo(body_mem_effects,
                                                                           parallel_stores,
                                                                           token_map))

            op.body.params = tuple(new_body_params)
            new_loop_op = dataclasses.replace(op, initial_values=tuple(new_initial_values),
                                              result_vars=tuple(new_result_vars))
            operations.append(new_loop_op)

            token_map = result_token_map

        elif isinstance(op, Continue):
            tokens = _get_cf_exit_tokens(innermost_loop_info.loop_mem_effects, token_map)

            new_continue_op = dataclasses.replace(op, values=tuple(op.values) + tokens)
            operations.append(new_continue_op)

        elif isinstance(op, Break):
            tokens = _get_cf_exit_tokens(innermost_loop_info.loop_mem_effects, token_map)

            new_break_op = dataclasses.replace(op, values=tuple(op.values) + tokens)
            operations.append(new_break_op)

        elif isinstance(op, IfElse):
            # Merge memory effects from both branches
            then_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[op.then_block]
            else_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[op.else_block]
            merged_mem_effects = then_mem_effects | else_mem_effects

            result_token_map = token_map.copy()
            new_result_vars = list(op.result_vars)

            def add_new_ifelse_result():
                x = _make_token_var(block.ctx, op.loc)
                new_result_vars.append(x)
                return x

            for alias_set, effect in merged_mem_effects.items():
                last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
                last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

                if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE:
                    continue
                elif effect == MemoryEffect.LOAD:
                    result_token_map[last_op_key] = add_new_ifelse_result()
                elif effect == MemoryEffect.STORE:
                    result_token_map[last_op_key] = add_new_ifelse_result()
                    result_token_map[last_store_key] = add_new_ifelse_result()
                else:
                    raise TileInternalError(f"Unexpected memory effect: {effect}")

            if merged_mem_effects.has_acquire_order:
                result_token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY] = add_new_ifelse_result()

            # Branch to then and else blocks
            for nested_block in op.nested_blocks:
                _to_token_order_in_block(nested_block, context, token_map.copy(),
                                         innermost_loop_info=innermost_loop_info,
                                         ifelse_info=IfElseInfo(merged_mem_effects))

            new_ifelse_op = dataclasses.replace(op, result_vars=tuple(new_result_vars))
            operations.append(new_ifelse_op)

            token_map = result_token_map

        elif isinstance(op, EndBranch):
            tokens = _get_cf_exit_tokens(ifelse_info.ifelse_mem_effects, token_map)

            new_end_branch_op = dataclasses.replace(op, outputs=tuple(op.outputs) + tokens)
            operations.append(new_end_branch_op)

        elif isinstance(op, TilePrintf):
            # Use ALIAS_UNIVERSE here to order print. This is a conservative solution that will use
            # the existing boop/branch token plumbing to preserve token order. Doing so could order
            # unrelated memory chains, which is acceptable for now.
            alias_set = ALIAS_UNIVERSE
            last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
            last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

            input_tok, maybe_input_tok_join_op = _get_input_token(last_op_key, op, token_map,
                                                                  None, block.ctx)
            if maybe_input_tok_join_op:
                operations.append(maybe_input_tok_join_op)

            [result_tok] = op.result_vars
            token_map[last_op_key] = result_tok
            token_map[last_store_key] = result_tok
            operations.append(dataclasses.replace(op, token=input_tok))
        else:
            operations.append(op)

    block.operations = operations


def _last_op_key(alias_set: AliasSet):
    return AliasTokenKey(alias_set, TokenRole.LAST_OP)


def _last_store_key(alias_set: AliasSet):
    return AliasTokenKey(alias_set, TokenRole.LAST_STORE)


def _make_token_var(ir_ctx: IRContext, loc: Loc) -> Var:
    var = ir_ctx.make_var("$token", loc)
    var.set_type(TokenTy())
    return var


def _collect_join_tokens(token_key: TokenKey,
                         token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
                         memory_order: MemoryOrder | None):

    def should_join(other_key):
        if other_key == ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY:
            return True

        assert isinstance(other_key, AliasTokenKey)

        mem_order_release_join = (memory_order is not None and
                                  memory_order_has_release(memory_order) and
                                  other_key.role == TokenRole.LAST_OP)
        alias_set_overlap_join = (other_key.role == token_key.role and
                                  (other_key.alias_set & token_key.alias_set))
        return mem_order_release_join or alias_set_overlap_join

    # Preserve the order of the tokens to join
    tokens_to_join = [token_map[token_key]]
    for other_key, other_tok in token_map.items():
        if not should_join(other_key):
            continue
        if other_tok not in tokens_to_join:
            tokens_to_join.append(other_tok)

    return tokens_to_join


def _get_input_token(token_key: TokenKey,
                     op: Operation,
                     token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
                     memory_order: MemoryOrder | None,
                     ctx: IRContext) -> Tuple[Var, Operation | None]:
    tokens_to_join = _collect_join_tokens(token_key, token_map, memory_order)

    if len(tokens_to_join) == 1:
        return tokens_to_join[0], None

    ret_tok = _make_token_var(ctx, op.loc)
    ret_op = JoinTokens(tokens=tuple(tokens_to_join), result_vars=(ret_tok,), loc=op.loc)
    return ret_tok, ret_op


def _get_cf_exit_tokens(cf_mem_effects: MemoryEffects,
                        token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var]) -> Tuple[Var, ...]:
    tokens = []
    for alias_set, effect in cf_mem_effects.items():
        last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
        last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

        if effect == MemoryEffect.NONE:
            continue
        if effect == MemoryEffect.LOAD:
            tokens.append(token_map[last_op_key])
        elif effect == MemoryEffect.STORE:
            tokens.extend((token_map[last_op_key], token_map[last_store_key]))

    if cf_mem_effects.has_acquire_order:
        tokens.append(token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY])

    return tuple(tokens)


# === LOOP PARALLEL STORE OPTIMIZATION ===


def _get_parallel_stores(
    loop_op: Loop,
    context: TokenOrderContext
) -> Set[Operation]:
    """
    A specific optimization for when there's only one TileStore
    on a given array in the for-loop body (including nested blocks),
    and the index doesn't overlap across iterations.
    We can parallelize the TileStore.

    Common in LayerNorm and RMSNorm patterns.
    """

    if not loop_op.is_for_loop:
        return set()

    # Skips this optimization if alias_set size > 1 is present in the loop body
    body_mem_effects = context.block_memory_effects[loop_op.body]
    if any(alias_set == ALIAS_UNIVERSE or alias_set.bit_count() > 1
           for alias_set, _ in body_mem_effects.items()):
        return set()

    nested_mem_effects = _get_nested_mem_effects(loop_op, context.block_memory_effects)

    alias_set_to_mem_ops = defaultdict(list)
    for op in loop_op.body.operations:
        if isinstance(op, (TileLoad, StorePointer, LoadPointer, TileStore,
                           TileAtomicCAS, TileAtomicRMW, TileAtomicRedView)):
            alias_set = context.dataflow_result[_get_input_var(op).name].alias_set
            alias_set_to_mem_ops[alias_set].append(op)

    tile_store_candidates = set()
    for alias_set, mem_ops in alias_set_to_mem_ops.items():
        if len(mem_ops) != 1:
            # More than 1 memory ops on the same array in loop
            continue
        elif not isinstance(mem_ops[0], TileStore):
            # The memory op on array is not TileStore
            continue
        elif nested_mem_effects[alias_set] != MemoryEffect.NONE:
            # The nested blocks / func calls have memory effects on array
            continue
        tile_store_candidates.add(mem_ops[0])

    # Filter in stores that have non-overlapping indices
    res = _filter_by_store_index(loop_op, tile_store_candidates, context.dataflow_result)
    return res


def _get_nested_mem_effects(
    loop_op: Loop,
    block_memory_effects: Dict[Block, MemoryEffects]
) -> MemoryEffects:
    nested_blocks = [b for op in loop_op.body.operations for b in op.nested_blocks]
    nested_mem_effects = EMPTY_MEMORY_EFFECTS
    for b in nested_blocks:
        nested_mem_effects = nested_mem_effects | block_memory_effects[b]
    return nested_mem_effects


def _filter_by_store_index(loop_op: Loop,
                           tile_store_candidates: Set[Operation],
                           dataflow_result: DataflowResult) -> Set[Operation]:

    def is_idx_injective(idx_var: Var) -> bool:
        # TODO: allow more complex injective check: j = i * 2 + 3
        return loop_op.is_for_loop and idx_var.name == loop_op.induction_var.name

    ret = set()
    for store_op in tile_store_candidates:
        ptr = _get_input_var(store_op)
        view_ty = store_op.view.get_type()
        if isinstance(view_ty, PartitionViewTy) \
                and not dataflow_result[ptr.name].may_alias_internally \
                and any(is_idx_injective(idx_var) for idx_var in store_op.index):
            ret.add(store_op)
    return ret


def _try_loop_parallel_store(
    store_op: TileStore,
    dataflow_result: DataflowResult,
    token_map: Dict[TokenKey, Var],
    innermost_loop_info: Optional[InnermostLoopInfo],
    ctx: IRContext,
) -> Optional[Tuple[Operation, ...] | Operation]:

    if (not innermost_loop_info or
            store_op not in innermost_loop_info.loop_parallel_stores):
        return None

    alias_set = dataflow_result[_get_input_var(store_op).name].alias_set
    last_op_key = _last_op_key(alias_set)
    last_store_key = _last_store_key(alias_set)

    # Convert to parallellized store
    parent_token_map = innermost_loop_info.parent_token_map
    before_loop_last_op_tok = parent_token_map[last_op_key]

    if (ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY in token_map and
            before_loop_last_op_tok is not token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY]):
        input_tok = _make_token_var(ctx, store_op.loc)
        maybe_input_tok_join_op = JoinTokens(
            tokens=(before_loop_last_op_tok, token_map[ACQUIRE_TOKEN_KEY]),
            result_vars=(input_tok,), loc=store_op.loc)
    else:
        input_tok = before_loop_last_op_tok
        maybe_input_tok_join_op = None

    [result_tok] = store_op.result_vars
    tko_store_op = dataclasses.replace(store_op, token=input_tok)

    # Eagerly join with loop_last_op_tok
    loop_last_op_tok = token_map[last_op_key]
    new_last_op_tok = _make_token_var(ctx, store_op.loc)
    join_op = JoinTokens(tokens=(loop_last_op_tok, result_tok),
                         result_vars=(new_last_op_tok,), loc=store_op.loc)

    token_map[last_op_key] = new_last_op_tok
    token_map[last_store_key] = new_last_op_tok

    return ((maybe_input_tok_join_op,) if maybe_input_tok_join_op else ()) + (tko_store_op, join_op)

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

import os


# Internal environment variables for debugging

CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR = os.environ.get('CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR', None)
CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE = os.environ.get('CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE', None)
CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_DIV = (
    os.environ.get("CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_DIV", "0") == "1")
CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_TOKEN_ORDER = (
    os.environ.get("CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_TOKEN_ORDER", "0") == "1")
# When 1, tileiras uses -O0, --device-debug, and a distinct disk cache key.
EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD = (
    os.environ.get("EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD", "0") == "1")

148	def check_rd_and_ftz(fn: str, rounding_mode: Optional[RoundingMode], flush_to_zero: bool,
149	                     dtype: datatype.DType):
150	    if rounding_mode is None and flush_to_zero is False:
151	        return
152	
153	    math_op_def = BINOP_REGISTRY[fn] if fn in BINOP_REGISTRY else UNARYOP_REGISTRY[fn]
154	    if rounding_mode is not None:
155	        if rounding_mode not in math_op_def.supported_rounding_modes:
156	            raise TileTypeError(
157	                f'Rounding mode {rounding_mode.value} is not supported for {fn}')
158	        min_version = math_op_def.supported_rounding_modes[rounding_mode]
159	        if min_version is not None:
160	            cur_version = Builder.get_current().ir_ctx.tileiras_version
161	            if cur_version < min_version:
162	                raise TileUnsupportedFeatureError(
163	                    f'{fn} rounding_mode={rounding_mode.value} requires tileiras '
164	                    f'{min_version.as_string()} or later. '
165	                    f'Current version is {cur_version.as_string()}.')
166	        if not datatype.is_unrestricted_float(dtype):
167	            raise TileTypeError(
168	                f'Rounding mode can only be used for unrestricted float types, '
169	                f'but got {dtype}')
170	        if rounding_mode in [RoundingMode.APPROX, RoundingMode.FULL]:
171	            if dtype != datatype.float32:
172	                raise TileTypeError(
173	                    f'Rounding mode {rounding_mode.value} can only be used for float32 type, '
174	                    f'but got {dtype}')
175	    if flush_to_zero:
176	        if not math_op_def.support_flush_to_zero:
177	            raise TileTypeError(f'Flush to zero is not supported for {fn}')
178	        if dtype != datatype.float32:
179	            raise TileTypeError(
180	                f'Flush to zero can only be used for float32 type, '
181	                f'but got {dtype}')

243	def _promote_dtype_and_loosely_typed_constant(dtype: DType,
244	                                              loose_const: Any,
245	                                              force_float: bool) -> DType:
246	    loose_dtype = dtype_of_constant_scalar(loose_const)
247	
248	    cat = datatype.numeric_dtype_category(dtype)
249	    if cat == NumericDTypeCategory.RestrictedFloat:
250	        # Treat restricted floats as regular floats.
251	        cat = NumericDTypeCategory.Float
252	    loose_cat = datatype.numeric_dtype_category(loose_dtype)
253	
254	    if loose_cat == cat:
255	        # Both values are of the same dtype category. Use the concrete dtype in this case.
256	        ret = dtype
257	
258	        # For integers, verify that the loosely typed constant is within the range of dtype.
259	        if cat == NumericDTypeCategory.Integral and not force_float:
260	            info = datatype.IntegerInfo(dtype)
261	            if not (info.min <= loose_const <= info.max):
262	                raise TileValueError(f"Integer constant {loose_const} is out of range of {dtype}")
263	    else:
264	        # Strongest category always wins
265	        ret = loose_dtype if loose_cat > cat else dtype
266	
267	    return ret if not force_float or datatype.is_float(ret) else datatype.default_float_type
268

270	def promote_dtypes(t1: DType | LooselyTypedScalar,
271	                   t2: DType | LooselyTypedScalar,
272	                   force_float: bool = False) -> DType:
273	    match t1, t2:
274	        case LooselyTypedScalar(val1), LooselyTypedScalar(val2):
275	            dtype1 = dtype_of_constant_scalar(val1)
276	            dtype2 = dtype_of_constant_scalar(val2)
277	            return _DTypePromotionImpl.promote_dtypes(dtype1, dtype2, force_float)
278	        case LooselyTypedScalar(val), dtype:
279	            return _promote_dtype_and_loosely_typed_constant(dtype, val, force_float)
280	        case dtype, LooselyTypedScalar(val):
281	            return _promote_dtype_and_loosely_typed_constant(dtype, val, force_float)
282	        case dtype1, dtype2:
283	            return _DTypePromotionImpl.promote_dtypes(dtype1, dtype2, force_float)
284	
285	
286	def promote_types(t1: TensorLikeTy | LooselyTypedScalar,
287	                  t2: TensorLikeTy | LooselyTypedScalar,
288	                  typing_hooks: TypingHooks,
289	                  force_float: bool = False) -> TensorLikeTy:
290	    dtype_1 = t1 if isinstance(t1, LooselyTypedScalar) else t1.tensor_dtype()
291	    dtype_2 = t2 if isinstance(t2, LooselyTypedScalar) else t2.tensor_dtype()
292	    dtype = promote_dtypes(dtype_1, dtype_2, force_float)
293	    shape = broadcast_shapes2(t1.tensor_shape(), t2.tensor_shape())
294	    return typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(dtype, shape)
295	
296	
297	class BroadcastError(Exception):
298	    pass
299	
300	
301	# FIXME: rename to broadcast_shapes() after we remove broadcast_shapes()
302	def broadcast_shapes2(s1: Sequence[int], s2: Sequence[int]) -> Tuple[int, ...]:
303	    result_shape = []
304	    for d1, d2 in itertools.zip_longest(reversed(s1), reversed(s2), fillvalue=1):
305	        if d1 != d2 and d1 != 1 and d2 != 1:
306	            raise BroadcastError(f"Shapes are not broadcastable: {tuple(s1)}, {tuple(s2)}")
307	        result_shape.append(max(d1, d2))
308	    return tuple(reversed(result_shape))
309	
310	
311	def is_shape_broadcastable_to(src: Sequence[int], dst: Sequence[int]) -> bool:
312	    return len(src) <= len(dst) and all(x in (y, 1) for x, y in zip(reversed(src), reversed(dst)))
313	
314	
315	def get_default_order(rank: int) -> tuple[int, ...]:
316	    return tuple(range(rank))

319	def validate_memory_order_and_scope(
320	    memory_order: MemoryOrder,
321	    memory_scope: MemoryScope,
322	    operation_type: type[Operation],
323	):
324	    opcode: str = operation_type._opcode
325	    if memory_order not in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_ORDERS:
326	        formatted_expected = ", ".join(
327	            str(order) for order in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_ORDERS
328	        )
329	        raise TileTypeError(
330	            f"Invalid memory order for {opcode}. "
331	            f"Got {memory_order}, expected one of {formatted_expected}"
332	        )
333	
334	    if memory_scope not in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_SCOPES:
335	        formatted_expected = ", ".join(
336	            str(scope) for scope in operation_type.VALID_MEMORY_SCOPES
337	        )
338	        raise TileTypeError(
339	            f"Invalid memory scope for {opcode}. "
340	            f"Got {memory_scope}, expected one of {formatted_expected}"
341	        )
342	
343	    if memory_order == MemoryOrder.WEAK and memory_scope != MemoryScope.NONE:
344	        raise TileTypeError(
345	            f"{opcode} with WEAK memory ordering cannot specify a memory scope"
346	        )
347	
348	    if memory_order != MemoryOrder.WEAK and memory_scope == MemoryScope.NONE:
349	        raise TileTypeError(
350	            f"{opcode} with {memory_order.name} memory ordering requires a memory scope"
351	        )

return (val,) * rank


@dataclass(eq=False)
class TileLoad(Operation, opcode="tile_load", memory_effect=MemoryEffect.LOAD):
    latency: Optional[int] = attribute()
    allow_tma: Optional[bool] = attribute()
    memory_order: MemoryOrder = attribute(default=MemoryOrder.WEAK)
    memory_scope: MemoryScope = attribute(default=MemoryScope.NONE)
    view: Var = operand()
    index: tuple[Var, ...] = operand()
    token: Optional[Var] = operand(default=None)

    VALID_MEMORY_ORDERS = (
        MemoryOrder.RELAXED, MemoryOrder.ACQUIRE, MemoryOrder.WEAK
    )

    VALID_MEMORY_SCOPES = (
        MemoryScope.NONE,
        MemoryScope.BLOCK,
        MemoryScope.DEVICE,
        MemoryScope.SYS,
    )

    @override
    def generate_bytecode(self, ctx: BytecodeContext) -> tuple[bc.Value, bc.Value]:
        tile_type: TileTy = self.result_vars[0].get_type()
        view_ty = self.view.get_type()
        keep_i64 = (isinstance(view_ty, PartitionViewTy)
                    and view_ty.array_ty.index_dtype.bitwidth > 32)
        res, res_token = bc.encode_LoadViewTkoOp(
            ctx.builder,
            tile_type=typeid(ctx.type_table, tile_type),
            result_token_type=ctx.type_table.Token,
            view=ctx.get_value(self.view),
            index=ctx.index_tuple(self.index, keep_i64=keep_i64),
            token=None if self.token is None else ctx.get_value(self.token),
            memory_ordering_semantics=memory_order_to_bytecode[self.memory_order],
            memory_scope=memory_scope_to_bytecode[self.memory_scope],
            optimization_hints=ctx.load_store_hints(self.latency, self.allow_tma),
            inbounds=_uniform_tuple(False, rank=len(self.index)),
        )
        return res, res_token


def _tile_load_impl_inner(array: Var, index_items: tuple[Var, ...], shape: Sequence[int],
                          order: Sequence[int], padding_mode: PaddingMode,
                          latency: Var, allow_tma: Var,
                          traversal_steps: Optional[tuple[int, ...]] = None,
                          memory_order: MemoryOrder = MemoryOrder.WEAK,
                          memory_scope: MemoryScope = MemoryScope.NONE) -> Var:
    array_ty = require_array_type(array)
    broadcasted_shape = (1,) * array_ty.ndim if len(shape) == 0 else shape
    latency = require_optional_constant_int(latency)
    allow_tma = require_optional_constant_bool(allow_tma)
    _check_load_store_hints(latency, allow_tma)

    # Promote indices to i64 for big arrays so that blockId * tileSize
    # doesn't overflow i32 in the backend's address computation.
    if array_ty.index_dtype.bitwidth > 32:
        index_items = tuple(astype(idx, array_ty.index_dtype) for idx in index_items)

    view = _materialize_tiled_view(array, broadcasted_shape, order, padding_mode,
                                   traversal_steps)
    res_ty = TileTy(array_ty.dtype, broadcasted_shape)
    result, _token = add_operation_variadic(TileLoad, (res_ty, TokenTy()),
                                            view=view, index=index_items, latency=latency,
                                            allow_tma=allow_tma, memory_order=memory_order,
                                            memory_scope=memory_scope)
    return reshape(result, shape)


@impl(ct.Array.get_raw_memory)
def get_raw_memory_impl(self: Var) -> Var:
    array_ty = require_array_type(self)

12	import cuda.tile as ct

17	
18	class TestMemoryBehavior:
19	
20	    def store_buffer(X, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
21	        tx0 = ct.arange(TILE, dtype=X.dtype)
22	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx0)
23	        # reverse so that each SIMT thread is less likely to be assigned the same address
24	        X_ALIAS = X
25	        reverse_offset = ct.arange(TILE, start=TILE - 1, step=-1, dtype=np.int32)
26	        tx1 = ct.gather(X_ALIAS, reverse_offset)
27	        ct.store(X_ALIAS, index=(0,), tile=tx1)
28	
29	    def store_buffer_alternative(X, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
30	        reverse_offset = ct.arange(TILE, start=TILE - 1, step=-1, dtype=np.int32)
31	        tx2 = ct.arange(TILE, dtype=X.dtype)
32	        ct.scatter(X, reverse_offset, tx2)
33	        tx3 = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
34	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx3)
35	
36	    def serialized_for_loop(X, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
37	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=ct.arange(TILE, dtype=X.dtype))
38	        # flip the buffer 3 times
39	        for i in range(3):
40	            reverse_offset = ct.arange(TILE, start=TILE - 1, step=-1, dtype=np.int32)
41	            tx = ct.gather(X, reverse_offset)
42	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx)
43	
44	    def serialized_while_loop(X, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
45	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=ct.arange(TILE, dtype=X.dtype))
46	        # flip the buffer 3 times
47	        i = 0
48	        while i < 3:
49	            reverse_offset = ct.arange(TILE, start=TILE - 1, step=-1, dtype=np.int32)
50	            tx = ct.gather(X, reverse_offset)
51	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx)
52	            i += 1
53	
54	    @staticmethod
55	    @ct.kernel
56	    def spinning_lock(X, L):
57	        while ct.atomic_cas(L, (), 0, 1, memory_order=ct.MemoryOrder.ACQUIRE) == 1:
58	            pass
59	        x_scalar = ct.gather(X, ())
60	        x_scalar += 1
61	        ct.scatter(X, (), x_scalar)
62	        ct.atomic_xchg(L, (), 0, memory_order=ct.MemoryOrder.RELEASE)
63	
64	    @pytest.mark.parametrize("kernel", [
65	        store_buffer, store_buffer_alternative,
66	        serialized_for_loop, serialized_while_loop
67	    ], ids=lambda f: f.__name__)
68	    def test_memory_behavior(self, kernel):
69	        tile_size = 1024

71	        expected = torch.flip(torch.arange(tile_size, device="cuda", dtype=torch.int32), [0])
72	        ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (1,), ct.kernel(kernel), (X, tile_size))
73	        torch.testing.assert_close(X, expected)

78	        L = torch.tensor(0, device="cuda", dtype=torch.int32)
79	        expected = X + n
80	        ct.launch(torch.cuda.current_stream(), (n,), self.spinning_lock, (X, L))
81	        torch.testing.assert_close(X, expected)

311	class TestForLoopParallelStoreMLIR(MLIRTestBase):
312	
313	    def parallel_store(X, _Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
314	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
315	        for i in range(n-1):
316	            ct.store(X, index=(i,), tile=tx)
317	        ct.store(X, index=(n-1,), tile=tx)
318	
319	    def two_parallel_stores(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
320	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
321	        range_n = range(n)
322	        for i in range_n:
323	            ct.store(X, index=(i,), tile=tx)
324	            ct.store(Y, index=(i,), tile=tx + 1)
325	
326	    def two_parallel_stores_with_nested_block(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
327	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
328	        for i in range(n):
329	            ct.store(X, index=(i,), tile=tx)
330	            for j in range(n):
331	                ct.store(Y, index=(j,), tile=tx + j)
332	
333	    def one_parallel_one_serial_store(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
334	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
335	        for i in range(n):
336	            # parallel store
337	            ct.store(X, index=(i,), tile=tx)
338	            ty = ct.load(Y, index=(i,), shape=(TILE,))
339	            # serial store
340	            ct.store(Y, index=(i,), tile=ty)
341	
342	    @override
343	    def compile_kernel(self, kernel):
344	        tile_size = 1024
345	        n = 10
346	        X = torch.arange(tile_size * 10, device="cuda", dtype=torch.int32)
347	        Y = torch.arange(tile_size * 10, device="cuda", dtype=torch.int32)
348	        bytecode = get_bytecode(kernel, (X, Y, n, tile_size))
349	        return bytecode
350	
351	    @pytest.mark.parametrize("kernel, check_directive", make_cases(
352	        (parallel_store, ForLoopParallelStoreCheckDirective),
353	        (two_parallel_stores, ForLoopTwoParallelStoresCheckDirective),
354	        (two_parallel_stores_with_nested_block,
355	         ForLoopTwoParallelStoresNestedCheckDirective),
356	        (one_parallel_one_serial_store,
357	         ForLoopOneParallelOneSerialStoreCheckDirective),
358	    ))
359	    @override
360	    def test_mlir(self, kernel, check_directive):
361	        super().test_mlir(kernel, check_directive)

387	class TestForLoopNonParallelStoreMLIR(MLIRTestBase):
388	
389	    def non_parallel_store_non_disjoint(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
390	        bidx = ct.bid(0)
391	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(bidx, 0), shape=(TILE, TILE))
392	        for i in range(n):
393	            ct.store(X, index=(bidx, i), tile=tx)
394	            ct.store(Y, index=(bidx, i), tile=tx + i)
395	
396	    def non_parallel_store_strided_view(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
397	        bidx = ct.bid(0)
398	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(bidx, 0), shape=(TILE, TILE))
399	        tv = Y.tiled_view(tile_shape=(TILE, TILE), traversal_steps=(TILE, 1))
400	        for i in range(n):
401	            tv.store(index=(bidx, i), tile=tx + i)
402	
403	    @override
404	    def compile_kernel(self, kernel):
405	        tile_size = 128
406	        n = 8
407	        # X, Y each of non-disjoint elements in memory
408	        X = torch.tensor(1., dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda").broadcast_to(
409	            (tile_size * n, tile_size * n))
410	        Y = torch.randn((tile_size * n, tile_size * n), device="cuda",
411	                        dtype=torch.float32)
412	        Y = torch.as_strided(Y, size=(tile_size * n, tile_size * n),
413	                             stride=(2, 1))
414	        bytecode = get_bytecode(kernel, (X, Y, n, tile_size))
415	        return bytecode
416

421	                     marks=[requires_tileiras(BytecodeVersion.V_13_3)]),
422	    ])

461	class TestWhileLoopMLIR(MLIRTestBase):
462	
463	    def while_loop_load(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
464	        tx1 = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
465	        i = 0
466	        while i < n:
467	            tx1 = ct.load(X, index=(i,), shape=(TILE,))
468	            i += 1
469	        tx2 = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
470	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx1 + tx2)
471	
472	    def while_loop_load_store(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
473	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
474	        i = 0
475	        while i < n:
476	            tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
477	            tx += 1
478	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx)
479	            i += 1
480	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx)
481	
482	    @override
483	    def compile_kernel(self, kernel):
484	        tile_size = 1024
485	        X = torch.arange(tile_size, device="cuda", dtype=torch.int32)
486	        bytecode = get_bytecode(kernel, (X, 10, tile_size))
487	        return bytecode
488

490	        (while_loop_load, WhileLoopLoadCheckDirective),
491	        (while_loop_load_store, WhileLoopLoadStoreCheckDirective),
492	    ))

736	class TestMemoryOrderMLIR(MLIRTestBase):
737	
738	    def make_no_control_flow_kernel(memory_order):
739	        @ct.kernel
740	        def no_control_flow_kernel(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
741	            tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
742	            y_scalar = ct.atomic_add(Y, 0, 1, memory_order=memory_order)
743	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
744	            ct.scatter(Y, 0, y_scalar + 1)
745	
746	        return no_control_flow_kernel
747	
748	    def make_if_else_kernel(memory_order: ct.MemoryOrder):
749	        @ct.kernel
750	        def if_else_kernel(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
751	            tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
752	            if n:
753	                y_scalar = ct.atomic_add(Y, 0, 1, memory_order=memory_order)
754	            else:
755	                y_scalar = ct.gather(Y, 0)
756	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
757	            ct.scatter(Y, 0, y_scalar + 1)
758	
759	        return if_else_kernel
760	
761	    def make_for_loop_kernel(memory_order: ct.MemoryOrder):
762	        @ct.kernel
763	        def for_loop_kernel(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
764	            tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
765	            for i in range(n):
766	                y_scalar = ct.atomic_add(Y, 0, 1, memory_order=memory_order)
767	                ct.scatter(Y, 0, y_scalar + 1)
768	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
769	
770	        return for_loop_kernel
771	
772	    def make_while_loop_kernel(memory_order: ct.MemoryOrder):
773	        @ct.kernel
774	        def while_loop_kernel(X, Y, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
775	            tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
776	            ct.atomic_add(X, 0, 1, memory_order=memory_order)
777	            i = 0
778	            while i < n:
779	                y_scalar = ct.atomic_add(Y, 0, 1, memory_order=memory_order)
780	                ct.scatter(Y, 0, y_scalar + 1)
781	                i += 1
782	            ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
783	
784	        return while_loop_kernel
785

787	        "no_control_flow_kernel": {
788	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELAXED: RelaxedNoControlFlowCheckDirective,

790	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELEASE: ReleaseNoControlFlowCheckDirective,
791	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQ_REL: AcqRelNoControlFlowCheckDirective,
792	        },
793	        "if_else_kernel": {
794	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELAXED: RelaxedIfElseCheckDirective,
795	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQUIRE: AcquireIfElseCheckDirective,
796	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELEASE: ReleaseIfElseCheckDirective,
797	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQ_REL: AcqRelIfElseCheckDirective,
798	        },
799	        "for_loop_kernel": {
800	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELAXED: RelaxedForLoopCheckDirective,
801	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQUIRE: AcquireForLoopCheckDirective,
802	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELEASE: ReleaseForLoopCheckDirective,
803	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQ_REL: AcqRelForLoopCheckDirective,
804	        },
805	        "while_loop_kernel": {
806	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELAXED: RelaxedWhileLoopCheckDirective,
807	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQUIRE: AcquireWhileLoopCheckDirective,
808	            ct.MemoryOrder.RELEASE: ReleaseWhileLoopCheckDirective,
809	            ct.MemoryOrder.ACQ_REL: AcqRelWhileLoopCheckDirective,
810	        }
811	    }

903	    def ifelse_alias(X, Y, _Z, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
904	        tx = ct.load(X, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
905	        if n:
906	            alias = Y
907	        else:
908	            alias = X
909	        ct.store(alias, index=(0,), tile=tx)
910	        ct.store(Y, index=(0,), tile=tx)
911	        ta = ct.load(alias, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
912	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=ta)
913	
914	    def for_loop_alias(X, Y, Z, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
915	        alias = X
916	        ct.store(alias, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 1, dtype=X.dtype))
917	        ct.store(Y, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 2, dtype=X.dtype))
918	        ta = ct.zeros((TILE,), dtype=X.dtype)
919	        for i in range(n):
920	            alias2 = alias
921	            ta = ct.load(alias2, index=(i,), shape=(TILE,))
922	            alias = Y
923	            ct.store(alias, index=(i,), tile=ta)
924	        ct.store(Y, index=(0,), tile=ta)
925	        ct.store(Z, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 3, dtype=X.dtype))
926	
927	    def while_loop_alias(X, Y, _Z, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
928	        alias = X
929	        ct.store(alias, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 1, dtype=X.dtype))
930	        ct.store(Y, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 2, dtype=X.dtype))
931	        i = 0
932	        while True:
933	            if i == n:
934	                break
935	            alias = Y
936	            i += 1
937	        ct.store(alias, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 2, dtype=X.dtype))
938	        ct.store(Y, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 3, dtype=X.dtype))
939	
940	    def control_flow_tuple_alias(X, Y, _Z, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
941	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 1, dtype=X.dtype))
942	        ct.store(Y, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 2, dtype=X.dtype))
943	        alias = Y
944	        for i in range(n):
945	            if i:
946	                alias = (Y, X)[0]
947	                ct.store(alias, index=(i,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 4, dtype=X.dtype))
948	            else:
949	                alias = Y
950	                ct.store(alias, index=(i,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 5, dtype=X.dtype))
951	        ty = ct.load(Y, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
952	        ct.store(alias, index=(0,), tile=ty)
953	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=ty)

1030	    def array_slice_load_store(X, _n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1031	        first_half = X.slice(axis=0, start=0, stop=TILE)
1032	        second_half = X.slice(axis=0, start=TILE, stop=2*TILE)
1033	        tx = ct.load(first_half, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1034	        ct.store(second_half, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
1035	
1036	        ty = ct.load(second_half, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1037	        ct.store(first_half, index=(0,), tile=ty)
1038	
1039	    def array_slice_for_loop(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1040	        for i in range(n):
1041	            chunk = X.slice(axis=0, start=i*TILE, stop=(i+1)*TILE)
1042	            tx = ct.load(chunk, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1043	            ct.store(chunk, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
1044	
1045	        first_chunk = X.slice(axis=0, start=0, stop=TILE)
1046	        ct.store(first_chunk, index=(0,), tile=ct.full((TILE,), 0, dtype=X.dtype))
1047	
1048	    def array_slice_ifelse(X, cond: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1049	        chunk = X.slice(axis=0, start=0, stop=TILE)
1050	        tx = ct.load(chunk, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1051	        if cond:
1052	            tx = ct.load(chunk, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1053	        ct.store(X, index=(0,), tile=tx)

1055	    def tiled_view_load_store(X, _n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1056	        tv = X.tiled_view(TILE)
1057	        tx = tv.load(0)
1058	        tv.store(1, tx + 1)
1059	        tv2 = X.tiled_view(TILE)
1060	        ty = tv2.load(1)
1061	        tv2.store(0, ty)
1062	
1063	    def tiled_view_for_loop(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1064	        tv = X.tiled_view(TILE)
1065	        for i in range(n):
1066	            tx = tv.load(i)
1067	            tv.store(i, tx + 1)
1068	        ct.store(X, (0,), ct.full((TILE,), 0, dtype=X.dtype))
1069	
1070	    def array_slice_while_loop(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1071	        chunk = X.slice(axis=0, start=0, stop=TILE)
1072	        tx = ct.load(chunk, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1073	        i = 0
1074	        while i < n:
1075	            tx = ct.load(chunk, index=(0,), shape=(TILE,))
1076	            ct.store(chunk, index=(0,), tile=tx + 1)
1077	            i += 1
1078	        ct.store(chunk, index=(0,), tile=tx)

1083	        if cond:
1084	            tx = tv.load(0)

1087	    def tiled_view_while_loop(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int]):
1088	        tv = X.tiled_view(TILE)
1089	        tx = tv.load(0)
1090	        i = 0
1091	        while i < n:

1097	    def tiled_view_strided_load_store(X, _n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int],
1098	                                      STEP: ct.Constant[int]):
1099	        tv = X.tiled_view(TILE, traversal_steps=STEP)
1100	        tx = tv.load(0)
1101	        tv.store(1, tx + 1)

1114	    def tiled_view_strided_while_loop(X, n: int, TILE: ct.Constant[int],
1115	                                      STEP: ct.Constant[int]):

=== memory_model.rst (head) ===
.. SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
..
.. SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

.. currentmodule:: cuda.tile

Memory Model
============

cuTile's memory model permits the compiler and hardware to reorder operations
for performance. Without explicit synchronization, the ordering of memory
accesses across |blocks| is not guaranteed.

To coordinate memory accesses between |blocks|, cuTile provides two attributes
for atomic operations:

*   **Memory Order** --- defines the ordering semantics of an atomic operation.
*   **Memory Scope** --- defines the set of |blocks| that participate in ordering.

Synchronization operates at per-element granularity: each element in the array participates
independently in the memory model.

For further details, see the Memory Model section of the `Tile IR documentation <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/tile-ir/>`_.

.. _memory-model-memory-order:

Memory Order
-------------------------

.. autoclass:: cuda.tile.MemoryOrder()
   :members:
   :undoc-members:
   :member-order: bysource

.. _memory-model-memory-scope:

Memory Scope
------------

.. autoclass:: cuda.tile.MemoryScope()
   :members:
   :undoc-members:
   :member-order: bysource
